lesson: Unit 02 Studio — MUS Unit Test II (Lessons 11–19)
banner: Week 22 · Final Review + MUS Unit Test II
essential_question: Can you show nine lessons of 2D-to-3D reasoning with nobody coaching you through it?
objective: Complete a final mixed review of Lessons 11–19, take Math-U-See Unit Test II independently, and reflect honestly on which lessons are mastered and which need reteaching.

content.prompt: Without notes, write one fact each about: circle parts, cylinder volume, a solid's surface area, simplifying a radical, and the Pythagorean theorem.

agenda.title: Review — Last Look Before the Test

content.heading: Fix what you missed
content.note: Go back over any missed problems from the capstone build and this week's practice, focusing on choosing the right volume/surface-area formula and simplifying radicals cleanly. Fix each one and note WHY you missed it.

content.heading: Show what you own — all nine lessons
On your own now
content.note: Take Math-U-See Unit Test II (Lessons 11–19) independently — no notes, no help. Put it in the folder for grading when you're done.

content.preview: Next week: Lesson 20, Special Right Triangles (45-45-90), starting Unit 03.

success_criteria.text: I can correctly answer mixed questions across Lessons 11–19 without help
I can complete MUS Unit Test II independently within one session
I can honestly rate my mastery of each of the nine lessons and name what needs reteaching

questions.prompt: The distance around a circle is called its…
questions.explain: Circumference is the distance around a circle, equal to 2πr.

questions.prompt: A solid with two parallel congruent circular bases connected by a curved surface is a…
questions.explain: A cylinder has two congruent circular bases joined by a curved lateral surface.

questions.prompt: A right triangle has legs 9 and 12. What's the hypotenuse?
Close: after MUS Unit Test II, rate your mastery of Unit 02 and name the ONE lesson from 11–19 you'd want to revisit.
